content.claim: Between 2010 and 2020, primary education SEN identification increased from 6.7% to 7.7%, and secondary education rose from 3.9% to 6.2%; separately, absolute numbers increased 1.6-fold over 20 years, but these metrics use different reference periods and sources remain unreconciled into a single harmonized time series.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Different metrics (share vs. absolute multiplier) from different reference periods
No single continuous time series across full 20+ years available
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
